methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $149M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Valar Atomics is a Series A–stage nuclear energy company that has raised $149M to date. The nuclear energy sector comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, and Kairos Power, though the private peer set lacks sufficient density at identical stage and valuation tier for robust median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, a single valuation method applies here: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($149M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement to later stages yields an implied valuation of $298M. This represents the median point in the triangulated range. The low-end scenario ($253M) and high-end scenario ($343M) reflect reasonable variance around this central estimate, though they derive from the same method and thus carry model risk rather than methodological diversity.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $253M to $343M, with $298M as the central point. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer comparables at identical stage and scale—limiting peer-median validation—and dependency on stage-progression multipliers that may not fully capture sector-specific risk or technology maturity. Additional clarity on unit economics, capital efficiency, and regulatory pathway would strengthen conviction in this range.
